hazard.summary: Quota or credit window exhaustion is surfaced as a throttling error but still enters a short retry loop, causing repeated retries despite no possibility of success until reset.
signals: Too many requests, please wait before trying again

constraints: Quota or credit exhaustion must not enter short retry loops
Terminal conditions must be surfaced to the user with reset semantics
Retry behavior must distinguish between transient throttling and quota exhaustion
Retry-after windows exceeding execution horizon must terminate the run
verification: Trigger a credit or quota exhaustion condition and observe system behavior
Verify system does not retry within short intervals (e.g. 10s)
Confirm terminal condition is surfaced with correct reset window
Ensure retry loop terminates instead of stalling execution
Validate user-facing state reflects blocked condition instead of active retry
notes: The system receives a clear provider signal indicating a 60-minute credit limit exhaustion, but continues retrying at short intervals as if the condition were transient throttling. This creates a false liveness state where execution appears active but cannot make progress. The failure is not absence of retry logic, but incorrect classification of a terminal condition as retryable.
